title: write_verilog: Vivado transparent memory port regression
body:
### Version

0.40

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

1. Emit Verilog code for memory with one write port and read port that is transparent to write port.

Code is emitted with https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/aa30589c123844bc576fc3c9938157b5db35a7c1/backends/verilog/verilog_backend.cc#L732 creating registered address input on read port.

Example:
```verilog
  reg [20:0] tag_mem_0 [127:0];
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (tag_mem_wp__en)
      tag_mem_0[tag_mem_wp__addr] <= tag_mem_wp__data;
  end
  reg [6:0] _2_;
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    _2_ <= tag_mem_rp__addr;
  end
  assign tag_mem_rp__data = tag_mem_0[_2_];
```

2. Synthesize it with Vivado

Tested on Vivado 2024.2
This issue comes from Amaranth language: https://github.com/amaranth-lang/amaranth/issues/1589

### Expected Behavior

Vivado synthesizes it correctly to block ram with transparency.

I assume it worked in previous Vivado versions, because this issue was not reported earlier, with successful Xilinx designs made.

### Actual Behavior

Vivado synthesis emits warning `The block RAM may get collision error if read and write address collide` and leads to non-functional gateware.

Workaround is to add `(* rw_addr_collision = "yes" *)` attribute for each memory declaration. Without it, the default behaviour is `design writes to the same address it is reading from, the RAM output is unpredictable`. Warnings are not emitted for non-transparent ports.
This is problematic for HDL generated code, that depends on verilog backend for external synthesis.
